county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created 1879 from Cragin County. Named for the city of Aurora. Agricultural county in the James River valley settled heavily by Scandinavian and German-Russian homesteaders.
county_metadata.research_tips: Aurora County Register of Deeds in Plankinton holds deed and land records from 1879. BLM General Land Office (GLO) records at glorecords.blm.gov document original homestead entries — essential since SD is a public land state. The South Dakota State Historical Society (SDSHS) in Pierre holds microfilmed county records. FamilySearch has microfilmed Aurora County deeds and vital records. German-Russian settlers from the Black Sea region arrived in the 1870s-1880s; check the Germans from Russia Heritage Society (GRHS) in Bismarck, ND for immigration records. Lutheran and Catholic church records document many Aurora County families. The Plankinton South Dakotan newspaper is on microfilm at SDSHS. Check Davison County Clerk of Courts for early probate records before Aurora County was fully organized.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Grantor/grantee deed books from 1879 to present. Register of Deeds maintains index books and recorded instruments.
Marriage licenses and certificates from 1879. Register of Deeds issued licenses; state registration began 1905.

source_profiles.source_name: Aurora County Clerk of Courts Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Probate, guardianship, adoption, naturalization, civil and criminal case files from 1879. Clerk of Courts maintains court records for the circuit court.

source_profiles.source_name: BLM General Land Office Records — Aurora County
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal land patents for Aurora County including homestead, preemption, timber culture, and cash entries. South Dakota was entirely public domain — GLO records are the primary source for first land ownership.
Federal population schedules for Aurora County. Dakota Territory enumerated from 1860. Agricultural schedules (1850-1880) provide additional family and economic data.

source_profiles.repository: South Dakota State Historical Society / Chronicling America
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Historical newspapers from Aurora County communities. SDSHS holds the largest collection of SD newspapers on microfilm. Chronicling America has digitized SD papers.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created 1879. County seat Huron was a major railroad hub and hosted the South Dakota State Fair. Beadle County was a center of Populist political activity in the 1890s.
county_metadata.research_tips: Beadle County Register of Deeds in Huron holds deed records from 1879. Huron was a key Chicago and North Western Railway town — railroad land sale records at the Newberry Library (Chicago) and Minnesota Historical Society identify early settlers. BLM GLO homestead records at glorecords.blm.gov cover original land entries. The Dakotaland Museum in Huron has local family files and photographs. Beadle County Clerk of Courts in Huron has probate, naturalization, and civil court records. The Huron Daily Plainsman newspaper (1882+) is an excellent genealogical source on microfilm at SDSHS. FamilySearch has microfilmed Beadle County records. The South Dakota State Fair was established in Huron in 1885 — fair premium lists and agricultural reports identify farming families. Strong Norwegian and Swedish settlement; check Scandinavian church records at Augustana University archives in Sioux Falls.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Grantor/grantee deed books from 1879 to present. Register of Deeds maintains index books and recorded instruments.
Marriage licenses and certificates from 1879. Register of Deeds issued licenses; state registration began 1905.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created 1909 from formerly unorganized territory on Pine Ridge Reservation. One of the last SD counties organized. Overlaps with portions of Pine Ridge Reservation.
county_metadata.research_tips: Bennett County Register of Deeds in Martin holds records from 1909 county organization. Bennett County overlaps Pine Ridge Reservation — BIA records at NARA Kansas City include Indian census rolls (1885-1940), allotment records under the Dawes Act, and school records for Oglala Lakota families. BLM GLO homestead records document non-Indian settlers who filed claims when reservation lands were opened. The Bennett County Historical Society in Martin has local family files. Clerk of Courts in Martin holds probate and civil records. The Todd County Tribune and Martin Messenger newspapers cover Bennett County news. Due to reservation overlap, researchers must consult both county and tribal/BIA records. Mixed-blood enrollment records at Pine Ridge Agency document intermarried families.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Grantor/grantee deed books from 1909 to present. Register of Deeds maintains index books and recorded instruments.
Marriage licenses and certificates from 1909. Register of Deeds issued licenses; state registration began 1905.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created 1862 as one of the original Dakota Territory counties. Named from French 'good man.' One of the earliest settled counties in SD; Bon Homme was an important Missouri River crossing point.
county_metadata.research_tips: Bon Homme County Register of Deeds in Tyndall holds some of South Dakota's oldest county land records, dating to 1862. As an original Dakota Territory county, early records may also be at SDSHS in Pierre. Czech (Bohemian) settlers were prominent — check Czech Catholic parish records (SS. Peter and Paul, Tabor) and the Tabor Czech Days collection at the local historical society. BLM GLO homestead records document original claims. The Missouri River formed the county's southern boundary; riverboat and ferry crossing records are at SDSHS. Bon Homme County Clerk of Courts has probate and naturalization records. FamilySearch has microfilmed county records. The Tyndall Tribune and Tabor newspapers are on microfilm at SDSHS. The Springfield area has early military records from Fort Randall (1856-1892); fort records are at NARA.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Grantor/grantee deed books from 1862 to present. Register of Deeds maintains index books and recorded instruments.
Marriage licenses and certificates from 1862. Register of Deeds issued licenses; state registration began 1905.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created 1862 as original Dakota Territory county. Home to South Dakota State University (SDSU, est. 1881). Important agricultural research center.
county_metadata.research_tips: Brookings County Register of Deeds in Brookings holds land records from 1862. South Dakota State University (SDSU) Archives and Special Collections has agricultural extension records, student records, and local manuscript collections. BLM GLO homestead records at glorecords.blm.gov. Norwegian and Swedish settlers predominated; Augustana-heritage Lutheran church records are essential — check Augustana University archives in Sioux Falls and ELCA archives in Chicago. Brookings County Clerk of Courts has probate, civil, and naturalization records. The Brookings Register newspaper (1879+) on microfilm at SDSHS documents community life. FamilySearch has microfilmed county deed and vital records. The county was one of the earliest organized in Dakota Territory. Brookings was a Chicago and North Western Railway division point — railroad employment records identify early residents.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Grantor/grantee deed books from 1862 to present. Register of Deeds maintains index books and recorded instruments.
Marriage licenses and certificates from 1862. Register of Deeds issued licenses; state registration began 1905.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created 1879. Aberdeen is the third-largest city in SD and a major railroad center. L. Frank Baum (author of Wizard of Oz) lived in Aberdeen 1888-1891.
county_metadata.research_tips: Brown County Register of Deeds in Aberdeen holds deed records from 1879. Aberdeen was a major Milwaukee Road and Chicago and North Western Railway junction — railroad records at the Milwaukee Road Historical Association and Newberry Library identify settlers recruited by railroad colonization agents. BLM GLO homestead records cover original land entries. Brown County Clerk of Courts in Aberdeen has extensive probate and naturalization records from the heavy immigrant settlement period. The Dacotah Prairie Museum in Aberdeen has local genealogy collections. L. Frank Baum published the Aberdeen Saturday Pioneer (1890-91) — issues are at SDSHS. Strong German-Russian (Black Sea Germans) settlement; check GRHS in Bismarck and German Evangelical church records. Aberdeen American News (1885+) is a key newspaper source on microfilm at SDSHS. Presentation College records document Catholic families.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Grantor/grantee deed books from 1879 to present. Register of Deeds maintains index books and recorded instruments.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created 1875 from unorganized territory. Named for the Brule (Sicangu) band of the Lakota Sioux. Chamberlain sits at the Missouri River crossing of I-90.
county_metadata.research_tips: Brule County Register of Deeds in Chamberlain holds land records from 1875. The Akta Lakota Museum and Cultural Center at St. Joseph's Indian School in Chamberlain has Native American historical materials. Missouri River crossing at Chamberlain was a major transportation point; early ferry and bridge records at SDSHS. BLM GLO homestead records document original entries. Brule County Clerk of Courts has probate and civil records. The county is named for the Sicangu (Brule) Lakota; neighboring Crow Creek and Lower Brule Reservations have BIA records at NARA Kansas City. Lutheran and Catholic church records serve the primarily Scandinavian and German settler families. The Chamberlain Register newspaper is on microfilm at SDSHS. FamilySearch has microfilmed county records.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created 1885. One of SD's least populated counties. Contains portions of Crow Creek Indian Reservation. Named for the American bison.
county_metadata.research_tips: Buffalo County Register of Deeds in Gann Valley holds land records from 1885. Buffalo County includes portions of Crow Creek Indian Reservation — BIA records at NARA Kansas City include census rolls, allotment records, and school records for Crow Creek Sioux families. BLM GLO homestead records document non-Indian settlers. Buffalo County is one of the least populated counties in the US. Clerk of Courts in Gann Valley has probate and civil records. Due to the small population, local newspaper coverage is limited; check the Gann Valley newspapers at SDSHS. FamilySearch has some microfilmed county records. Fort Thompson (Crow Creek Agency) records at NARA are essential for tribal families. The county's history is deeply intertwined with the Crow Creek Reservation.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created 1883 from Lawrence County. Belle Fourche was the geographic center of the nation until 1959 (Alaska). Major cattle ranching and sheep country.
county_metadata.research_tips: Butte County Register of Deeds in Belle Fourche holds deed records from 1883. Belle Fourche was a major cattle shipping point on the railroad — livestock brand records at the SD Brand Board and SD Stockgrowers Association identify ranching families. BLM GLO homestead records document original land entries. Butte County Clerk of Courts has probate, civil, and naturalization records. The Tri-State Museum in Belle Fourche has local history collections spanning the SD-WY-MT border region. The Belle Fourche Bee newspaper (1896+) is on microfilm at SDSHS. Cattle ranching records and stock inspection reports at SDSHS document the open range era. Lawrence County holds pre-1883 records for this area. FamilySearch has microfilmed county records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Grantor/grantee deed books from 1883 to present. Register of Deeds maintains index books and recorded instruments.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created 1873. Named for Norman B. Campbell, early Dakota Territory legislator. Located along the border with North Dakota; includes portions of Standing Rock Reservation.
county_metadata.research_tips: Campbell County Register of Deeds in Mound City holds land records from 1873. The county borders North Dakota and includes portions of Standing Rock Reservation — BIA Standing Rock Agency records at NARA Kansas City include census rolls, allotment records, and agency correspondence for Hunkpapa and Yanktonai Lakota/Dakota families. BLM GLO homestead records document settlers who filed claims. Campbell County Clerk of Courts in Mound City has probate and civil records. Strong German-Russian settlement; check GRHS in Bismarck, ND and Evangelical church records. The Mound City newspapers at SDSHS cover community history. Standing Rock Reservation crosses the ND-SD border — check both states' records. FamilySearch has some microfilmed county records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Grantor/grantee deed books from 1873 to present. Register of Deeds maintains index books and recorded instruments.
